text.title: pytest Fixtures — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every python-pytest-fixtures application must obey.

rule.statement: Pick the narrowest scope that meets the need: function (default) > class > module > session. Widening scope is a perf optimisation, not a default.
rule.rationale: Wider scopes share state across tests and create order-dependency; narrow scopes are the safe default.

rule.statement: Use yield for fixtures with cleanup. Code after yield runs even when the test fails.
rule.rationale: Return-style fixtures cannot clean up; yield gives setUp/tearDown semantics in a single function.

rule.statement: When tests need different variants of the same object, use a factory fixture (returns a callable), not multiple fixtures.
rule.rationale: Factory fixture parametrises creation; avoids combinatorial fixture explosion.

rule.statement: Use autouse=True only for fixtures that should affect every test transparently (e.g., reset_app_state). Never on heavy fixtures.
rule.rationale: Autouse on a heavy fixture multiplies its cost across the whole suite; explicit injection is preferable.

rule.statement: Fixtures depend on other fixtures by declaring them as parameters. The fixture graph is the test's setup recipe.
rule.rationale: Composition keeps each fixture single-purpose; pytest resolves the graph automatically.

rule.statement: Never store mutable state at module scope inside a fixture without explicit reset/teardown.
rule.rationale: Mutable shared state causes order-dependent test failures and intermittent CI flakes.
